message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): mpsc::recv mid-flight cancel via select-race pattern (tick 432)

Finding + contract pin for a previously-undocumented gap: asupersync
mpsc::Receiver::recv(cx) observes **pre-cancel** via its per-poll
cx.checkpoint() short-circuit (tick 422 pins that), but does NOT
register a cx-cancel-waker. A recv already suspended on the send-side
waker will NOT wake when cx.cancel_with(...) fires afterwards.

This was discovered during tick 432 while attempting a naive
mid-flight cancel test: a 2 s outer wrapper's timeout fired before
the recv observed the 100 ms mid-flight cancel. Same class of gap
as ft-l9mxa (HTTP client mid-flight cancel, resolved by the tick 387
race_with_cx_cancel helper on DistributedHttpClient) and the
sleep_with_cx / timeout_with_cx primitive docs (ticks 328/331) that
document primitives observing BUDGET but not DIRECT CANCEL.

The new test pins the caller-side WORKING pattern: wrap the recv in a
`futures::future::select` against a poll-sleep cx-cancel watcher.
The watcher branch catches the cancel within its 50 ms poll interval,
surfacing as `Either::Right(Err)` within ~150 ms of the cancel firing.

The match-arm structure ALSO tolerates a future improvement in
asupersync where mpsc.recv itself gains cx-cancel-waker support —
the test accepts `Either::Left(Err(mpsc::RecvError::Cancelled))` as
an alternative valid outcome, so the test stays green if the
underlying observability improves.

Test name chosen (`_via_select_race_pattern`) to make the caller-
pattern nature explicit; distinguishes from the tick 422 pre-cancel
test (which pins the primitive-level pre-cancel short-circuit).

Observed locally: 0.18 s wall total — cancel fires at 100 ms, watcher
observes within the next 50 ms poll cycle, test returns.

Check script Run 6/6 filter `mpsc_recv_with_cx` already catches this
new test alongside the pre-cancel test — no script change needed.
Run 6 grows 16 → 17 tests. Total: 103 → 104.

Full check script passes locally via fork bypass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
